quote: Kirkland Signature generated $90 billion in sales in FY2025, representing roughly one-third of Costco's $270 billion total merchandise sales: According to Costco's 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders, Kirkland Signature brought in about $90 billion in sales in 2025, a $15 billion increase vs. 2024's $86 billion. This makes Kirkland larger than all but a few CPG giants (Nestle, P&G, PepsiCo, Unilever).

Kirkland Signature has grown from launch (1995, ~24 items) to $90B in 30 years, consistently outpacing overall Costco sales growth: Launched October 1995 with about two dozen items (cookies, juice, diapers, sweaters, tires). Replaced ~24 separate private-label brands. FY2021: $59B (31% of revenue). FY2024: $86B. FY2025: $90B. In Q3 FY2025, Kirkland sales outpaced overall sales growth, lifting brand penetration by 50 basis points YoY.

Costco stocks only ~4,000 SKUs total, of which Kirkland Signature represents ~400-600 products — radical curation vs. supermarket's 30,000-40,000: Costco stocks approximately 3,700-4,000 active SKUs. Kirkland Signature covers around 400-600 products globally (sources vary on exact count). A typical supermarket carries 30,000-40,000 items. Walmart carries 120,000+. Costco has 1,000+ Kirkland trademarks filed worldwide.

Kirkland's quality standard is 'equal or better than national brands' at 20%+ savings, with strict markup caps (14% regular items, 15% Kirkland): Company rule: no regular item marked up more than 14% over cost, no Kirkland item more than 15%. Target: 11% on total COGS. Kirkland must be 'equal or better quality than national brands' with at least 20% price savings. CFO Millerchip Q2 2026: 'KS items typically offering 15% to 20% value compared to the national brand alternative, with equal or better quality.'

Private-label products carry gross margins 5-8 percentage points above equivalent national brands, making Kirkland Costco's margin engine: Private labels carry gross margins 5 to 8 percentage points above equivalent national brands. Kirkland delivers 15%+ margins, nearly double national brands on the same shelf. Costco's operating margin (14.2%) outpaces Walmart's (5.4%) and Amazon's (5.1%). Gross margin FY2026 Q1: 12.88%, up from 12.67% in FY2025 Q2.

Costco's Kirkland supplier model: partners with premium national brand manufacturers (Starbucks, Duracell), who produce Kirkland versions — eliminating middlemen: Costco gives brand-name suppliers the chance to make the Kirkland version. Examples: Starbucks roasts Kirkland coffee, Duracell produces Kirkland batteries, Einstein Bros makes Kirkland bagels. Costco has abandoned Kirkland categories (cosmetics, soda, toothpaste) when national brands perform significantly better. CEO Ron Vachris personally signs off on each new Kirkland addition.

Costco vertically integrates key supply chains — built its own poultry processing plant in 2017 to control the chicken supply chain end-to-end: Costco went all-in on poultry by setting up their own chicken processing plant in 2017. By owning the whole chicken supply chain, Costco dodges price hikes and keeps rotisserie chicken at $4.99. In 2026, Costco is increasing domestic sourcing for Kirkland to counter tariffs.

Costco's limited SKU strategy produces extraordinary capital efficiency: $850K+ revenue per employee (vs. $348K industry average), 33.7% ROIC (vs. Walmart's 17.1%): Each U.S. employee contributes an estimated $850,000+ in annual revenue, whereas the warehouse club and supercenter industry average is about $348,000 — over double the sales per employee. Costco's ROIC stands at 33.7% versus Walmart's 17.1%. The limited SKU approach drives faster inventory turns and less handling.

Costco's 4,000 SKUs allocate 3/4 to food and daily necessities (high-frequency, essential) and 1/4 to discretionary categories (apparel, electronics, toys): Costco将仅有的4000个SKU中的3/4都分配给了食物和日常必须品等高频、刚需类商品，仅有1/4分配给服饰家居、电器玩具等更低频、可选的商品。This 75/25 split ensures the curated assortment covers core household needs while maintaining the 'treasure hunt' appeal.

In Q2 2026, Costco launched 30 new Kirkland items and is accelerating domestic sourcing to offset tariff pressures while protecting price gaps vs. national brands: CEO Ron Vachris: company leaning in on Kirkland because that's where Costco has 'the most control of the supply chain.' CFO Millerchip: 'We are continuing to work on supply chain efficiencies, and Kirkland Signature penetration continues to improve.' 30 new items launched in Q2 2026 alone. Increasing domestic sourcing to address tariff-related cost pressures.

Duan Yongping explicitly compared Xinpinmu to the Costco model and predicted it would be 'quite formidable' in 10 years with ~4,000 SKUs: Duan Yongping's exact quote on Xueqiu: '这个其实就是Costco的样子。拼多多如果线上品牌只有4000个左右的SKU的话，10年后会是相当厉害的。' Translation: 'This is essentially the Costco model. If PDD's online brand has around 4,000 SKUs, it will be quite formidable in 10 years.' He drew the parallel to Costco's first decade (1983-1993) where it focused on refining 4,000 SKUs rather than rapid expansion.

Duan Yongping proposed a 'brand licensing' model: PDD supervises quality and licenses the Xinpinmu brand to qualified factories, rather than operating factories itself: Duan's view paraphrased: '拼多多不需要抢客户生意，只要扶植爆品公司就好，监督质量，达到标准就可以把自己的品牌授权给厂家。只需要选出某些有代表性的产品用来打自己的品牌就可以了。' Translation: 'PDD doesn't need to steal merchants' business, just nurture hit-product companies, supervise quality, and license its brand to qualified factories. It only needs to select certain representative products to build its own brand.'

4,000 SKUs covers approximately 80% of high-frequency household consumption needs, creating a closed-loop consumer experience: Analysis from TMTPost: 'From a coverage dimension, 4,000 SKUs correspond to the core radius of household essentials. Under this structure, 4,000 SKUs can cover approximately 80% of high-frequency consumption needs, forming a relatively complete consumption closed loop.' This shifts user behavior from multi-platform price comparison to single-platform decision-making.

Duan Yongping's '10 years' mirrors Costco's own foundational decade (1983-1993) when it refined selection, compressed costs, stabilized quality, and built trust before scaling: TMTPost analysis: 'Costco自1983年成立后的第一个10年，并未急于开店，而是围绕4000个SKU打磨选品、压缩成本、稳定质量、建立信任。段永平所说的10年，并非时间本身，而是强调优质商业模式需要时间锤炼。' Translation: 'In its first 10 years after founding in 1983, Costco didn't rush to open stores but instead refined 4,000 SKUs, compressed costs, stabilized quality, and built trust. Duan's 10 years is not about the time itself but emphasizing that quality business models need time to be forged.'

Duan Yongping holds 7.75 million PDD shares (worth $906M) as of Q1 2025, making it his third-largest position — his endorsement is backed by significant capital commitment: As of Q1 2025, Duan Yongping's H&H International Investment held 7.75 million PDD shares, market value exceeding $900 million, his third-largest holding. He increased his position by 908,800 shares (11.72% increase) in Q2. He was PDD founder Huang Zheng's angel investor and mentor.

Xinpinmu = Costco's curated model + PDD's traffic-matching efficiency + China's supply chain responsiveness — a fundamentally different proposition from JD's distributor model: Multiple analyst summaries converge on this formula. Guancha analysis: 'Xinpinmu will adopt a selective approach: only choose certain representative products to build its own brands, not all commodities.' TMTPost: '新拼姆品牌=Costco的精选模式+PDD式流量匹配效率+Made in China的供应链响应.' This is brand creation (like Kirkland), not inventory distribution (like JD).

Xinpinmu announced March 25, 2026: CNY 15B initial injection, CNY 100B over 3 years, dedicated Shanghai entity, integrating Pinduoduo + Temu supply chains for self-owned brands: PDD co-chair Zhao Jiazhen: 'This year marks a critical window for China's supply chain transformation and Pinduoduo will mobilize all resources to promote Xinpinmu' to achieve 'a high-quality, domestically self-owned brand within three years.' Name etymology: '拼' from Pinduoduo, '姆' from Temu. Dedicated company established in Shanghai. Initial cash injection: CNY 15B ($2.1B). Three-year total: CNY 100B ($14.5B).

JD's self-operated model is DISTRIBUTOR (buys existing brands, resells) — fundamentally different from Xinpinmu's BRAND CREATOR model: JD自营: buys inventory from existing brands (Samsung, Nike, P&G), manages logistics and customer service, acts as an 'online retailer.' JD owns the inventory but NOT the brand. 京东自营是卖别人的品牌 (JD self-operated sells others' brands). JD Jingzao (京造, launched 2018) exists as a store brand (~2,000 products, Muji-style household goods) but remains a tiny side project relative to JD's core 1P business.

Xinpinmu's operational model: platform defines product specs, quality standards, and designs; factories execute production to blueprint — brand ownership stays with PDD: Ichaoqi analysis: '过去是工厂生产什么，平台卖什么。现在是平台说：我要这个，你照着做' (Previously factories determined production; now the platform says: I want this, you produce it). Factories receive complete product blueprints including design specs, technical standards, production parameters, quality benchmarks. The resulting brands belong to Pinduoduo, not factories. Factories receive stable processing fees.

Xinpinmu is a LIGHTER-asset version of Kirkland: brand licensing/authorization to qualified factories rather than Costco's heavy buy-and-hold inventory model: TMTPost/Guancha analysis: 'Costco采用的是重资产买断模式，需要提前压货、承担库存风险。而新拼姆更可能走向一种轻资产的授权模式：平台制定标准、负责质量监管与品牌背书，生产则交由工厂完成。' Translation: 'Costco uses a heavy-asset buy-out model requiring advance inventory. Xinpinmu will more likely move toward a light-asset authorization model: platform sets standards, handles quality supervision and brand endorsement, while production is delegated to factories.'

Xinpinmu leverages Temu's 3 years of global consumer data to define products BEFORE manufacturing — progressing from C2M advisory to direct production specification: Ichaoqi: 'Xinpinmu leverages Temu's three-year global consumer data accumulation to define products before manufacturing begins. This represents progression from C2M (consumer-to-manufacturer) advisory relationships toward direct production specification — platforms become product architects rather than data providers.'

Three concrete Xinpinmu measures: (1) dedicated company + CNY 100B for industrial cluster coverage, (2) teams embedded in production clusters for digital upgrading, (3) brand export solutions including warehousing, IP, and legal support: Sina Finance: '新拼姆将推出三项具体措施：成立专项公司，未来三年投入千亿元覆盖国内产业带，培育自营品牌；组建团队深入产业带，提供数字化方案，推动制造标准升级；推出品牌出海解决方案，覆盖仓储物流、知产服务、法律支持等环节。'

PDD's Costco DNA is not new — Huang Zheng positioned PDD as 'Costco + Disney' at its 2018 IPO; Xinpinmu is the most literal execution of that vision to date: Multiple sources confirm: '拼多多在2018年上市时就提出，拼多多应该是Costco和迪士尼的结合体。' PDD's original thesis was combining Costco's curated value proposition with Disney's entertainment/engagement model. Xinpinmu represents the fullest realization of the Costco half of that equation.

Skeptics warn Xinpinmu faces real risks: self-operated is fundamentally heavier than platform, Thrasio's failure is a cautionary tale, and balancing with existing merchants is a governance challenge: Skeptical view: '做自营和做平台完全是两个概念。自己做品牌，要面对高库存风险和非常复杂的全链路管理，当年估值百亿的亚马逊品牌收购巨头Thrasio就是被规模包袱拖到破产的。' Also: '自营体系与平台原有商家的利益平衡、供应链资源的分配，考验着集团的组织协同能力.' The shift from pure platform to brand owner is structurally risky.

PDD sits on RMB 422.3 billion ($60.4B) in cash and short-term investments — the financial fortress to fund Xinpinmu's CNY 100B investment over 3 years: PDD's cash position as of latest earnings: RMB 422.3 billion ($60.4 billion). The CNY 100B Xinpinmu commitment represents roughly 24% of PDD's total cash pile, deployed over three years — significant but not existentially risky.

Xinpinmu transforms PDD's revenue model from pure platform (ad revenue + commissions) to a hybrid 'platform + self-operated' model — the most fundamental business model shift since PDD's founding.: Pre-Xinpinmu revenue mix (Q4 2025): Online marketing services (ads): RMB 60.01B (+5% YoY); Transaction services (commissions + Temu): RMB 63.90B (+19% YoY). Ad revenue growth decelerated to 5% — near structural ceiling. Post-Xinpinmu: adds direct product revenue from self-operated brands, shifting from commission-based to margin-based retail on branded products. Chinese media: '不搞自营、光靠收商家买流量钱和交易佣金过活的拼多多，曾是三大电商里最赚的。但现在，拼多多忽然说要搞重资产自营了' (PDD that never did self-operation and lived on ad/commission revenue was the most profitable of the big three — but now it suddenly says it will do heavy-asset self-operation).

Xinpinmu's brand licensing model differs fundamentally from JD's self-operated model and Amazon Basics: PDD owns the brand, sets standards, but licenses production to factories — staying 'asset-medium' rather than going fully asset-heavy.: Duan Yongping's framework: PDD should 'select representative products, create its own brand, and license the brand to factories while overseeing quality — not competing with merchants.' This creates a 'single-SKU authorization model' where PDD provides 'standard answers' (standardized branded products) while merchants handle 'subjective questions' (non-standard, personalized products). This is lighter than JD's full inventory/warehousing model but heavier than pure marketplace. One analyst described Xinpinmu as 'a Costco belonging to Pinduoduo' — curated high-volume products at extreme value rather than comprehensive category coverage.

The merchant relationship is designed as complementary rather than competitive: Xinpinmu focuses on standardized commodity products while merchants handle non-standard/personalized goods. However, tension is acknowledged as a real risk.: Complementary design: 'Platform provides standard answers (标品), merchants do subjective questions (非标/个性).' Xinpinmu uses 'fee waivers + direct support' (减免+扶持) to help merchants upgrade rather than displacing them. BUT contradicting concern: 'How to balance self-operated brands with third-party merchants is a huge test. The market also questions whether PDD's inherent low-price image is compatible with low-price-high-quality self-operated brand positioning.'

Xinpinmu integrates both Pinduoduo domestic and Temu international supply chain resources, targeting global markets — not just domestic. This addresses Temu's key weakness: consumers buy products but don't remember brands.: Xinpinmu integrates '拼多多+Temu的强大供应链资源' to 'systematically incubate and self-operate brands facing different countries and categories.' Key insight: 'merchants achieved product export but struggled establishing brand recognition in consumers' minds' — Xinpinmu solves this by creating memorable platform-owned brands. This means Xinpinmu brands would be sold through both Pinduoduo (domestic) and Temu (international) channels, leveraging global consumer data for 'made-to-order' and 'precision R&D.'

The capital intensity shift is significant but manageable: RMB 100B over 3 years against RMB 422B cash pile. Short-term margin pressure is explicitly expected and accepted by management.: Investment: RMB 100B over 3 years (RMB 15B first tranche). Cash: RMB 422.3B as of Dec 2025. Annual investment run rate (~RMB 33B/year) is approximately 33% of FY2025 net profit (RMB 99.3B). CFO Liu Jun: 'the external environment and competitive landscape are undergoing rapid changes, and the company must continue to explore and make investments. These investments are firm and long-term and will inevitably impact financial performance.' Management: 'future profit margins will continue to fluctuate across quarters — this is the new normal.'

Xinpinmu requires new organizational capabilities: dedicated teams deployed to industrial clusters for product standardization, quality assurance, IP management, and brand marketing — capabilities PDD never needed as a pure platform.: Three core Xinpinmu measures: (1) Dedicated company with RMB 100B to deploy customized manufacturing solutions across industrial clusters; (2) Specialized teams going deep into manufacturing hubs for digital transformation, product/tech/marketing integration; (3) Comprehensive brand export solutions covering product standards, warehousing logistics, IP services, and legal assistance. This is a massive organizational expansion from PDD's famously lean ~20,000-person structure.

PDD's C2M strategy has evolved through four distinct phases: (1) New Brand Alliance 1.0 in 2018, (2) Upgraded New Brand Alliance 2.0 in 2020, (3) Temu international expansion 2022-2025, and (4) Xinpinmu self-operated brands in 2026.: Phase 1 (2018): 'New Brand Plan' (新品牌计划) launched to support 1,000 factories with R&D guidance, big data, and traffic. Phase 2 (2020): Upgraded to 2.0 with targets of 100 industrial clusters, 100,000 custom products, and RMB 1 trillion in sales by 2025; partners expanded from 1,000 to 5,000; added Hundred Billion Subsidy resources. Phase 3 (2022-2025): Temu expansion to 90+ countries in 3 years. Phase 4 (2026): Xinpinmu — platform-owned brands, RMB 100B investment over 3 years.

PDD's C2M model created concrete results before Xinpinmu: by 2020, C2M orders had reached 460 million, and multiple factory brands (Beiyingshuang, Kailin, Jiaweishi, Sanhe) had exceeded RMB 100M annual sales on the platform.: PDD's New Brand Plan had accumulated 4.6 billion C2M orders by 2020. Factory-to-consumer brands like 贝婴爽 (baby care), 凯琴 (appliances), 家卫士 (home cleaning), 三禾 (cookware) each achieved annual sales exceeding RMB 100M. PDD also co-created the '蓓丽' brand with Pechoin (百雀羚).

Xinpinmu marks a qualitative shift from PDD's prior C2M work: the platform moves from 'helping factories build their own brands' to 'owning the brand and licensing it to factories' — from enabler to brand owner.: Previous model: PDD provided traffic, data, and subsidies; factories retained brand ownership. Xinpinmu model: PDD creates and owns the brands, sets quality standards, and authorizes factories to produce under PDD's brand. Duan Yongping: 'PDD doesn't need to compete with merchants — it should select representative products, brand them, and license the brand to factories while overseeing quality.'

The C2M evolution reveals a consistent strategic arc: PDD spent 7 years (2018-2025) building deep relationships with Chinese industrial clusters before taking the step of owning brands — Xinpinmu is not a pivot but the culmination of this groundwork.: Central University of Finance and Economics professor Liu Chunsheng: '拼多多正试图从供应链搬运工转向对供应链的深度嵌入与重塑' (PDD is shifting from supply chain 'mover' to deep embedding and reshaping of the supply chain). This is described as leveraging 'industrial leverage' through deep penetration of industrial clusters. The dedicated Xinpinmu teams have already deployed to agriculture regions and manufacturing hubs.

The 'low price + high quality' positioning is inherently paradoxical, and PDD's existing low-price brand image may undermine premium brand-building efforts.: Market skepticism: '拼多多要做低价高质的新拼姆，而低价高质这词本身就是个悖论' (PDD wants low-price-high-quality Xinpinmu, but 'low-price-high-quality' is itself a paradox). PDD's brand has been associated with counterfeit goods and extreme discounting — building consumer trust in PDD-owned brands faces an uphill battle. This is the opposite problem from Costco, whose brand connotes reliability.

Despite the 'complementary' framing, Xinpinmu will inevitably compete with existing merchants on standardized products — some analysts warn merchants will be 'depressed' by this development.: Article headline: '拼多多一年狂赚近千亿，但商家接下来要郁闷了' (PDD earned nearly RMB 100B in profit, but merchants will be depressed going forward). Duan Yongping himself acknowledged the competitive tension: 'When merchants compete on price, will some deliberately lower product quality to cut costs? This is a systemic, thorny problem.' The authorization model only works if PDD strictly limits its branded SKUs to avoid cannibalizing merchant sales.

PDD is going against industry trends — competitors like Alibaba and ByteDance are investing heavily in AI and food delivery, while PDD is uniquely choosing supply chain/brand ownership. This is either contrarian genius or a strategic misstep.: Yicai Global noted that Xinpinmu 'represents a significant strategic shift as PDD chooses to forge its own path amidst industry trends that heavily emphasize artificial intelligence and food delivery.' While PDD allocated 30% more to R&D (RMB 16.5B) in 2025, this is primarily for supply chain technology rather than AI consumer products. The divergence from industry consensus is notable.

Duan Yongping's 10-year timeline for Xinpinmu to become 'formidable' implies this is a very long-term bet with uncertain intermediate milestones — investors expecting near-term returns may be disappointed.: Duan Yongping: '10年后会是相当厉害的' (formidable in 10 years). Costco itself took 10 years to refine its 4,000 SKU model. PDD's 3-year 'rebuild another PDD' target vs. Duan's 10-year outlook creates a tension between management's ambitious timeline and an experienced investor's more conservative estimate.

The RMB 100B investment, while manageable relative to PDD's cash pile, represents a shift from the company's historically capital-light, high-margin model to a capital-intensive approach — exactly the kind of model PDD was founded to disrupt.: PDD was celebrated as the most profitable of China's big-three e-commerce platforms precisely because it avoided self-operation. FY2025 net profit already declined 12% before Xinpinmu spending begins. The annual ~RMB 33B investment run-rate equals roughly one-third of annual profit. PDD's pitch to investors was always 'asset-light platform with operating leverage' — Xinpinmu reverses this narrative.

Huang Zheng articulated PDD's founding vision as 'Costco + Disney' in his IPO letter dated June 30, 2018, describing Pinduoduo as a combination of high-quality products at good prices (Costco) with entertainment/fun (Disney), driven by distributed intelligent agent networks.: Huang Zheng's IPO shareholder letter (2018-06-30): 'It will be a Costco and Disney (combining high-quality products at good prices with entertainment) driven by distributed intelligent agent networks rather than centralized AI systems.' He positioned Pinduoduo as 'a platform creating a multi-dimensional space where virtual networks and the real world merge, enabling users to purchase affordably while gaining happiness.'

Huang Zheng admitted in June 2018 that the Disney component was still nascent, comparing PDD to having built 'only a merry-go-round' in what aspired to be a Disneyland.: Huang Zheng to media (2018-06): '就好像我们想造一个迪士尼乐园，但是现在只造了一个旋转木马，还处在比较初级的阶段' (We want to build Disneyland, but we've only built a merry-go-round — still at a very early stage).

In his 2021 farewell shareholder letter, Huang Zheng reiterated the Costco+Disney vision, saying it would become 'more concrete and vivid', while acknowledging PDD was still in the 'early stage' of multi-value plus multi-fun.: Huang Zheng 2021 shareholder letter: 'Costco+Disney的愿景将会更具象、更生动地展现在面前。虽然当前还只是多实惠、多乐趣的初级阶段，但我们已经能够感受到多实惠叠加上多乐趣后带来的性质性的微妙的不同。'

By 2023, PDD had quietly shifted its positioning from 'multi-value + multi-fun' (多实惠+多乐趣) to 'multi-value + good service' (多实惠+好服务), effectively deprioritizing the 'Disney' component in favor of operational excellence.: As of Q1 2023 earnings, PDD adjusted its positioning from '多实惠+多乐趣' to '多实惠+好服务'. This shift coincided with the post-Huang Zheng era under Chen Lei's leadership.

Xinpinmu represents the fulfillment of the 'Costco' half of Huang Zheng's founding vision — Duan Yongping explicitly called it 'essentially Costco's model' and predicted it would be 'remarkably powerful in 10 years' with ~4,000 SKUs.: Duan Yongping on Xueqiu (2026-03): '这个其实就是Costco的样子。拼多多如果线上品牌只有4000个左右的SKU的话，10年后会是相当厉害的。' He further explained that PDD need not compete with merchants — it should select representative products for its own brand and license the brand to factories while overseeing quality.

Duan Yongping's Costco parallel is grounded in Costco's first-decade strategy: Costco spent its first 10 years (1983-1993) not aggressively expanding but polishing ~4,000 SKUs, compressing costs, stabilizing quality, and building trust — exactly what he predicts for Xinpinmu.: Duan Yongping analyzed that Costco 1983-1993 spent a decade refining 4,000 SKUs rather than rushing to open stores. He proposes Xinpinmu should follow the same discipline: 'only select representative products for its brand, authorize the brand to factories, and oversee quality standards.'

Chinese manufacturing is structurally trapped at the bottom of the 'smiling curve' — the overall operating margin for China's industrial enterprises above designated size was just 5.31% in 2025, while branded consumer products globally achieve 45-70% gross margins.: China NBS data (2025): Above-designated-size industrial enterprises had operating revenue profit margin of 5.31%, down 0.03pp YoY. Total revenue: RMB 139.2 trillion, total profit: RMB 7,398.2 billion (+0.6% YoY). For OEM/contract manufacturing specifically, net margins are typically 3-5%. By contrast, branded consumer products globally achieve 45-70% gross margins — a 2-3x gap. Private label fashion brands achieve up to 65% gross margins vs 25-35% for third-party sellers.

Costco's Kirkland Signature demonstrates the private-label margin premium: Kirkland products carry ~15% margins (nearly double national brands' ~7-8%), generate 32-35% of total sales from just 12.5% of SKUs, while pricing 15-20% below national brands for consumers.: Costco CFO Gary Millerchip (Q2 2026 earnings): 'Kirkland Signature items typically offering 15% to 20% value compared to the national brand alternative, with equal or better quality.' Kirkland generates 32-35% of Costco revenue from ~500 products (12.5% of assortment). Kirkland margins ~15% vs national brands ~7-8%. Costco's overall operating margin (14.2%) outpaces Walmart (5.4%) and Amazon (5.1%). Analysts project Kirkland reaching 35-40% of sales by 2030.

The 'smiling curve' concept — with manufacturing trapped at the value trough while R&D/design and branding/marketing capture disproportionate value — directly motivated Xinpinmu. Chinese media frames this as PDD 'taking back pricing power' (拿回定价权).: The smiling curve (proposed by Acer founder Stan Shih in early 1990s) shows manufacturing/assembly at the profit trough. For Chinese industrial cluster merchants: 'they possess world-class manufacturing capabilities but often earn only meager processing fees.' Xinpinmu is PDD's solution to this structural problem — 'the profit margins of midstream assembly and manufacturing will no longer be compressed but rather elevated by economies of scale and brand premium.' Ministry of Commerce researcher Hong Yong: 'through building brand advantages, products can form differentiation and premium capacity, shifting from price competition to value competition.'

Chinese companies that successfully moved up the smiling curve — Anta, Huawei, Xiaomi — demonstrate the feasibility but also the long timeframe required. Only 2 Chinese brands (Huawei at $117.8B, Xiaomi) made the 2026 Interbrand Global Top 100.: Anta: transformed from Jinjiang OEM factory to Official Olympic Committee sportswear supplier. Huawei: brand value $117.8B, top-10 globally in 2026 World Brand 500. Xiaomi: $131.5B market cap, pursuing 'high-end strategy' as core direction. However, 78 Chinese companies in World Brand 500 averaged only $29.2B per brand vs US levels — 'large but not strong' pattern persists. Only 2 Chinese brands in Interbrand Global 100.

PDD's revenue growth decelerated sharply from 131% (Q1 2024) to 10% (FY2025), with quarterly growth hitting single digits (7-9%) in mid-2025, creating urgent need for a new growth engine.: Revenue growth trajectory: Q1 2024: 131%, Q2 2024: 86%, Q3 2024: 44%, Q4 2024: 24%, Q1 2025: 10%, Q2 2025: 7%, Q3 2025: 9%, Q4 2025: 12%. FY2025 revenue: RMB 4,318B (+10% YoY). FY2025 net profit: RMB 993B (-12% YoY, non-GAAP).

PDD's domestic user base has essentially plateaued at ~872 million, leaving virtually no room for user-driven growth. This forces the company to seek revenue per user and value chain improvements.: PDD domestic user base: 872 million as of Q1 2025, growing only 3 million over two years. With China's internet population around 1.1 billion, PDD has penetrated approximately 80% of the addressable market.

De minimis tariff elimination in May 2025 devastated Temu's US business (sales -23% YoY, DAU -58%), forcing PDD to rethink its cross-border model from direct-from-China shipping to local warehousing with branded products.: US de minimis exemption eliminated August 29, 2025 (announced May 2025). Impact on Temu: US sales down 23% YoY in May 2025, DAU dropped 58%. Temu pivoted from 70% fully-managed to under 30%, built 13 self-owned warehouses globally (10 Europe, 2 US, 1 Mexico). Goldman Sachs estimates Temu 2026 global GMV will exceed $100B. Temu's US GMV share dropped below 40% of total.

PDD accumulated RMB 422.3 billion in cash/equivalents/short-term investments by end of 2025, up 27% YoY — providing massive firepower for the RMB 100B Xinpinmu investment without external financing.: Cash position as of Dec 31, 2025: RMB 422.3B (vs RMB 331.6B at end of 2024, +27% YoY). The RMB 100B three-year Xinpinmu commitment represents approximately 24% of total cash reserves. First tranche of RMB 15B is just 3.6% of cash.

PDD's gross margin compressed from peak levels to 56.3%, total costs surged 23% YoY (to RMB 188.8B), and R&D spending jumped 30% (to RMB 16.5B) — confirming the company entered a deliberate reinvestment phase before Xinpinmu was even announced.: FY2025 financials: Total costs RMB 188.8B (+23% YoY, exceeding revenue growth of 10%). R&D: RMB 16.5B (+30%). Sales & marketing: RMB 125.3B (+13%). Net profit non-GAAP declined 12% YoY. Management statement: 'future profit margins will continue to fluctuate across quarters — this is the new normal.'

The announcement was paired with earnings on March 25, 2026, because PDD's governance restructure in December 2025 — making Zhao Jiazhen co-chairman/co-CEO — was the prerequisite. Zhao's mandate was explicitly 'three years to rebuild Pinduoduo.': December 2025: PDD annual shareholder meeting appointed Zhao Jiazhen as co-chairman and co-CEO alongside Chen Lei. Zhao immediately issued 'focus, heavy investment in supply chain upgrade, high-quality development' as the group strategy, with the explicit target of '力争三年再造一个拼多多' (strive to rebuild another PDD in three years). The Q4 2025 earnings release (March 25, 2026) was the first earnings under this new governance structure.

PDD internal consensus is that 2026 is the 'critical window' for Chinese supply chain transformation — making this a now-or-never moment to capture manufacturing capacity being displaced by tariff disruptions and geopolitical shifts.: Zhao Jiazhen: '2026年国内供应链迎来了转型升级的关键窗口期' (2026 marks the critical window for domestic supply chain transformation and upgrade). Context: tariff wars have disrupted global trade patterns, Chinese factories face 'double squeeze' from developed country re-industrialization and developing country competition, and excess manufacturing capacity needs new outlets.

Kirkland Signature generated $90 billion in sales in fiscal year 2025, accounting for roughly one-third of Costco's $270 billion total merchandise sales: According to Costco's 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders, Kirkland Signature brought in about $90 billion in sales in FY2025 (ended Aug 31, 2025), a $15B+ increase year-over-year. Around 600 products carry the Kirkland label globally.

Kirkland suppliers are overwhelmingly major national brand manufacturers who simultaneously sell their own branded products on Costco shelves — creating a dual-manufacturing model, not a displacement model: Duracell makes Kirkland batteries (confirmed by CEO Craig Jelinek, 2016). Kimberly-Clark (Huggies) makes Kirkland diapers (confirmed by CFO Richard Galanti, 2017). Starbucks roasts Kirkland coffee (branded 'Custom roasted by Starbucks'). Reynolds makes Kirkland aluminum foil (dual-branded). Bumble Bee makes Kirkland tuna. These suppliers produce both their own brand AND Kirkland simultaneously.

Despite dual manufacturing, there IS tension: national brands are not pleased when Kirkland launches competing products, and brands like Kind, Planters, Pampers have been edged out by Kirkland alternatives: CFO Richard Galanti acknowledged: 'Costco hasn't lost any relationships with suppliers by launching its own Kirkland products, but those brands are not usually pleased when Costco introduces one.' Brands edged out include Kind, Planters, Kimberly-Clark's Huggies, and P&G's Pampers in certain categories. In 2025, Lululemon sued Costco over alleged Kirkland dupes of its apparel.

Costco's Kirkland model is NOT a profit-sharing arrangement — Costco caps markup at 14-15% and passes savings to members. Suppliers accept because Costco's volume is too large to ignore: CFO Gary Millerchip in Q2 2026 earnings call: Kirkland items offer '15% to 20% value compared to the national brand alternative, with equal or better quality.' Costco maintains markup cap of 14% (15% for KS). Suppliers participate because Costco is too big a revenue stream to ignore, even though they're effectively creating their own competition.

Kirkland's market share is accelerating: private-label brands now command 20.7% of US retail spending (record share), and Costco is expanding Kirkland aggressively in 2026 as a tariff hedge: Costco launched ~30 new Kirkland items in Q2 2026 alone. CEO Ron Vachris confirmed it 'makes sense to lean in on Kirkland Signature because that's where Costco has the most control of the supply chain.' Since 2021, private-label unit sales increased >2% while national brand unit sales declined ~7%.

Media analysis warns PDD's small/medium merchants WILL be most impacted by Xinpinmu, as their already-thin margins face direct competition from a platform-backed brand with superior resources: Sina Finance analysis (March 26, 2026): '拼多多上的中小型商家，将会受到最大的冲击，尤其拼多多上的商家，利润本就有限。如果商家选择提价应对，那拼多多有可能丧失低价优势。' Also: '如何平衡自营品牌与第三方商家之间的竞争，也是一个巨大考验。'

The 'referee and player' (既是裁判又是运动员) concern is being raised for Xinpinmu, echoing criticism historically leveled at JD.com and Amazon — but PDD's model may differ structurally: Multiple media outlets raised the question: '拼多多是要从裁判员变运动员，做无数个品牌？还是因为名字里带姆，走精选的路子，做电商版的山姆？' This debate frames the key tension. However, PDD's response via Duan Yongping's interpretation is that this is brand licensing, not inventory-holding distribution.

JD.com's self-operated model provides a cautionary example: self-operated products get preferential search placement, JD uses buying power for cost advantages, and recently drew regulatory action for restricting merchants' cross-platform activity: JD self-operated products consistently placed at top of search results regardless of price/quality. JD self-operated to POP GMV ratio was 7:3, management target is 4:6. In Oct 2025 Double-11, JD set up patrol teams monitoring merchant Douyin livestreams, fining brands like Midea RMB 5M for 'price violations.' In March 2026, Beijing regulators formally summoned JD and 11 other platforms for 'infringing merchants' autonomous operating rights.'

Amazon's private label (Amazon Basics) model is the anti-pattern: FTC antitrust case alleges Amazon used 3P seller data to develop competing products, used anti-discounting algorithms to manipulate prices, and coerced FBA usage. Trial set for October 2026: FTC sued Amazon Sept 2023 with 19 states. Key allegations: (1) Used third-party seller data to develop private label competitors, (2) 'Buy Box Suppression' algorithm suppresses sellers pricing lower on competitor sites, (3) 'Project Nessie' algorithm raised prices yielding $1.4B in excess profits. Amazon settled with EU in 2022, agreeing not to use 3P seller data for private label development. Trial date: October 13, 2026.

MOFCOM researcher Hongyong views Xinpinmu as shifting competition from price to value — helping industrial belts escape commodity competition through brand differentiation, which is structurally complementary rather than competitive: 商务部研究院电子商务研究所研究员洪勇: '通过打造品牌优势，商品有望形成差异化和溢价能力，从价格竞争转变为价值竞争，引领产业带走出同质化竞争的局面，实现品牌化发展。' This government researcher's view supports the complementary interpretation.

Temu's experience validates the need for branding: strong supply chain but 'goods without brand' (有货无牌) weakness. Xinpinmu is positioned to solve Temu's brand gap, not compete with domestic merchants: Analysis: 'Temu的实践既验证了中国供应链在全球市场的竞争力，也暴露了有货无牌的短板。新拼姆要补的正是这块短板。' Temu operates in 90+ countries. The brand gap in global markets is distinct from domestic merchant competition — this is about giving Chinese factories global brand power, not undermining domestic marketplace merchants.

THREE DISTINCT SELF-OPERATED MODELS exist with radically different merchant conflict profiles: (1) JD distributor model — high conflict, (2) Amazon Basics private label — high conflict + antitrust risk, (3) Kirkland/Xinpinmu brand licensor — potentially lower conflict: Model 1 (JD): Buy inventory from existing brands, resell via owned logistics. Compete with 3P merchants on same SKUs. Result: search preference for self-operated, merchant fines for cross-platform pricing, regulatory action. Model 2 (Amazon): Use platform data to identify categories, create competing private-label products. Result: FTC antitrust suit, EU settlement, $1.4B excess profit allegation. Model 3 (Kirkland/Xinpinmu): Partner with factories to produce under platform brand. Factory remains the producer and beneficiary. Result: tension exists but is structurally different — the factory IS the supplier, not a displaced competitor.

KEY DIFFERENCE: In JD/Amazon models, existing merchants are DISPLACED. In Kirkland/Xinpinmu model, existing factories are UPGRADED — they become branded suppliers rather than losing business to a platform competitor: Costco: Duracell makes Kirkland batteries AND sells Duracell on same shelves. PDD vision (Duan Yongping): '扶植爆品公司就好，监督质量，达到标准就可以把自己的品牌授权给厂家' — nurture hit-product companies, license brand to factories that meet standards. The factory GAINS brand power rather than losing market share. However, this is the ASPIRATIONAL model — execution may differ. Costco's own experience shows national brands DO lose shelf space over time.

CRITICAL CAVEAT: Even Kirkland, the 'gentler' model, creates real tension — brands like Kind, Planters, Pampers have been displaced. The key question for Xinpinmu is whether the PER-SKU authorization model can limit conflict better than category-wide private label: Costco's Kirkland pressure on national brands is well-documented. LaunchPad Inc. founder Jeremy Smith advises clients: 'Assume a KS version of your product will come into the market.' Lululemon sued Costco in 2025 over Kirkland dupes. However, PDD's proposed 'per-SKU authorization' model differs: authorization targets individual products, not categories. Platform handles 'standard answers' (标品), merchants handle 'non-standard/personalized' (非标). If executed this way, conflict is structurally limited to standardizable commodity products.

Xinpinmu was formally announced March 25, 2026 alongside PDD's 2025 Q4/full-year earnings. Two companies registered in Shanghai in early February 2026 with combined RMB 150 billion registered capital: 上海新拼姆鸿乔电子商务有限公司 registered Feb 2, 2026 in Changning District, registered capital RMB 100B. 上海新拼姆朴动电子商务有限公司 registered Feb 3, 2026 in Pudong, registered capital RMB 50B. Total first-tranche: RMB 150B cash. Three-year plan: RMB 1,000B total investment.

Co-CEO Zhao Jiazhen positioned Xinpinmu as integrating Pinduoduo + Temu supply chain resources to systematically incubate self-operated brands for global markets — 'three years to recreate another PDD': Zhao Jiazhen: '2026年国内供应链迎来了转型升级的关键窗口期，拼多多将举全集团之力，公司上下力出一孔，争取在三年内推动新拼姆实现国产自营品牌的高质量转型，带动中国供应链实现转型升级和价值跃迁。' Zhao was named co-chairman + co-CEO on Dec 19, 2025.

Xinpinmu's three core measures: (1) specialized company with customized manufacturing solutions across industrial belts, (2) dedicated teams embedded in production clusters for end-to-end support, (3) comprehensive brand-going-global solutions: Three measures announced: 其一，成立专项公司推出定制化制造方案；其二，组建专项团队深入产业带提供一体化方案；其三，推出全方位的品牌出海解决方案。This extends PDD's existing 新质供给 program with formal corporate structure and massive capital commitment.

PDD 2025 full-year revenue was RMB 431.8B (+10% YoY), but net profit declined 12% to RMB 99.4B due to heavy investment — sales & marketing expenses RMB 125.3B (+13%), R&D expenses RMB 16.5B (+30%): Q4 2025 revenue: RMB 123.9B. Q4 net profit: RMB 24.5B (-11% YoY). Full-year net profit: RMB 99.4B (-12%). CFO Liu Jun warned investments 'will inevitably impact financial performance.' This signals profit sacrifice for strategic repositioning.

PDD's 新质供给 (New Quality Supply) program, launched April 2025 with RMB 1,000B in funding + traffic resources, is the PRECURSOR to Xinpinmu — Xinpinmu is its corporate evolution, not a brand-new initiative: In April 2025, PDD launched '千亿扶持' (Thousand Billion Support Plan), committing RMB 1,000B+ over three years in capital and traffic resources to push industrial belts toward '新质供给'. First batch of 1,000+ industrial belt merchants saw: average order value UP 47%, repeat purchase rate jumped from 23% to 40%.

PDD's 新质供给 teams have embedded in 100+ manufacturing clusters across China, working at raw material and component levels — this is operational depth that competitors lack: 新质供给 teams visited: 义乌美妆与饰品 (Yiwu cosmetics/jewelry), 深圳数码 (Shenzhen electronics), 山东零食 (Shandong snacks), 邵东箱包 (Shaodong bags), 平湖羽绒 (Pinghu down), 威海钓具 (Weihai fishing gear), 惠州零食, 佛山童装 (Foshan children's wear), 曹县汉服 (Caoxi hanfu), among 100+ clusters.

Shaodong bags case study: produced 70%+ of China's student backpacks but was trapped in OEM mode. Under PDD's data-driven product development support, multiple hit brands emerged, transforming from 'OEM hinterland' to 'brand highland': 邵东生产了全国七成以上的学生书包，但长期以代工为主。在拼多多的扶持下，当地商家借助平台的数据分析，进行精准的产品研发，先后涌现出了多个爆款品牌，推动邵东完成了从'代工腹地'向'品牌高地'的产业转变。

Factory-level case studies show PDD empowerment model in action: Yiwu ice tray maker went from low-margin commodity to RMB 60M annual sales through micro-innovation; Weihai fishing rod makers used carbon fiber cost reduction to create competitive brands: 义乌95后张小杰 (Yiwu Gen-Z entrepreneur): used platform data for product micro-innovation, developed a press-type ice cube tray that achieved RMB 60M annual sales. 威海'钓迷'品牌创始人张荣波: leveraged carbon fiber material cost drops to develop RMB 100-level fishing rods. '创威'品牌创始人孙中文: built own carbon cloth factory, used e-commerce sales data for reverse-customization of raw materials, compressed inventory lead time from 45 days to 15 days.

Pinghu down jacket factory case: grew annual PDD sales from RMB 10M (2021) to RMB 47M (2024) through dual strategy of licensed brand (雅鹿) + own brand development. Factory owner praised PDD's simple rules and factory-friendly model: 平湖本土企业'艾菩诺鸶'，通过拿下'雅鹿'品牌授权与打造自主品牌'两条腿'走路，在拼多多上的年销售额从2021年的1000万元增长至2024年的4700万元。深刻感受到平台规则简洁、对工厂友好、运营成本低的红利。

PDD platform merchant demographics show rapid growth: Gen-Z (post-95) merchants grew 31% YoY, post-00 merchants grew 44% YoY, quality product SKUs grew 50%+ YoY: 平台产业带商家数量快速增长，95后商家数量同比增长31%，00后商家数量同比增长44%，优质产品SKU数量同比增长超五成。This suggests PDD is attracting new factory merchants, not displacing existing ones.

Air freight per-parcel cost from China to US is $6-$10 via consolidated methods, vs ocean freight at $0.10-$0.30/kg for dense cargo in containers: Air freight: standard $5-$8/kg, express $8-$12/kg, small parcels under 1kg $15-$30. Consolidated/hybrid air freight: $6-$10 per parcel. Ocean FCL: $2,100-$5,000 per 40ft container (China to US), translating to ~$0.10-$0.30/kg for dense cargo. Ocean LCL: $0.50-$1.00/kg. Air freight is 4-6x more expensive than ocean per unit.

Temu's bulk shipping to UK warehouses reduced per-item logistics costs by 40-60% compared to air freight: Temu reports that bulk shipping to UK third-party warehouses has reduced per-item logistics costs by 40-60% compared to traditional air freight, while also improving delivery speeds from weeks to days.

Cross-border air freight from China to US declined ~50% after de minimis revocation, with projections of 90% decline at 145% tariff rates: E-commerce accounted for ~50% of China-to-US airfreight volume in 2024. ~100,000 tons/month of de minimis traffic moving across Pacific. One firm estimated ~90% reduction in parcels at 145% tariffs. Air rates from Hong Kong to US spiked 50% to $7.95/kg in mid-April 2025 rush before May 2 deadline.

Industry consensus: permanent shift from air freight to ocean shipping for Chinese e-commerce: 'We can expect to see these companies begin shipping most items in bulk rather than single parcels, which will reduce speed to market and increase the amount of inventory held in the U.S. This could mean a permanent shift from air freight to ocean shipping.' - industry expert quoted by FreightWaves

Landed cost comparison for a typical $10 product: old Temu model vs bulk import model: OLD MODEL (cross-border parcel): Product $10 + air freight ~$7/parcel + tariff 54% ($5.40) + $100 per-parcel fee = ~$122.40 landed cost. BULK IMPORT MODEL: Product $10 + ocean freight ~$0.50/unit + standard Section 301 tariff ~25% ($2.50) + no per-parcel fee + US warehouse pick/pack ~$3 = ~$16 landed cost. The per-parcel fee alone makes the old model uneconomical. MATH: Even at the trade-truce 54% rate, the $100 per-parcel postal fee is the killer for sub-$20 items.

EU Council agreed to impose EUR 3 flat customs duty on all parcels under EUR 150, effective July 1, 2026: On December 12, 2025, the European Council formally agreed to a fixed EUR 3 customs duty per item category on small parcels entering the EU from July 1, 2026. The duty applies to each distinct tariff sub-heading in a parcel (e.g., silk blouse + wool blouse = EUR 6). Covers IOSS-registered non-EU sellers who account for 93% of cross-border e-commerce imports.

EU will add an additional EUR 2 handling fee per consignment starting November 2026, and EUR 0.50 for warehouse-routed parcels: European Commission proposed EUR 2 handling fee for direct-to-consumer parcels from outside the EU, and EUR 0.50 for parcels first routed to EU warehouses before shipping to end consumers. This creates a direct financial incentive for the warehouse-first (bulk import) model. Combined impact: EUR 3 duty + EUR 2 handling fee = EUR 5 per parcel for direct cross-border; EUR 3 duty + EUR 0.50 = EUR 3.50 for warehouse-routed.

EUR 3 flat duty is temporary; from 2028 all imports under EUR 150 will face standard tariff-based duties when EU Customs Data Hub launches: The EUR 3 duty is explicitly temporary (July 2026 - July 2028, extendable). When the EU Customs Data Hub becomes operational, the EUR 150 threshold disappears entirely and all imports will be subject to standard product-level tariff rates. EU standard tariff rates on Chinese goods range from 10-20% depending on category.

EUR 3 flat duty destroys margins on ultra-low-price items (EUR 20 product example: profit wiped out): A EUR 20 trinket with ~15% margin (EUR 3 profit) loses all profit after EUR 2 handling fee + EUR 2+ customs duty. 91% of small shipments into the EU originate from China. 4.6 billion such packages entered the EU in 2024, nearly double 2023 figures. 65% of parcels are estimated to be undervalued to fall below the EUR 150 line.

EU regulatory design explicitly incentivizes warehouse-first model over direct cross-border shipping: The EUR 2 direct-to-consumer handling fee vs EUR 0.50 warehouse-routed fee creates a EUR 1.50/parcel cost advantage for the bulk-import-to-warehouse model. This regulatory design directly rewards the Xinpinmu-type value chain (factory -> bulk ship -> EU warehouse -> local delivery) over the old Temu model (factory -> air parcel -> direct to consumer).

US eliminated de minimis exemption for China/HK imports effective May 2, 2025, ending Temu's zero-tariff model: Executive order signed April 2, 2025. All postal items from China/HK under $800 now subject to duty: initially 30% or $25/item (rising to $50 after June 1). Later escalated to $100/item (May 2) then $200/item (June 1). Post-trade deal (May 14, 2025): 54% tariff on formerly de-minimis goods, or $100 per postal parcel.

US eliminated de minimis for ALL countries effective August 29, 2025, with per-item duties based on IEEPA tariff rate brackets: Global de minimis rates: $80/item for countries with <16% IEEPA rate, $160/item for 16-25%, $200/item for >25%. From February 28, 2026 onward, only ad valorem methodology (not per-item flat fee) may be used.

A $10 Temu item that cost $10 pre-tariff now costs $15.40-$24.50 depending on rate regime: Pre-May 2025: $10 item = $10 final cost (0% tariff via de minimis). May 2025 at 120-145%: $10 item = $22-$24.50. Trade truce at 54%: $10 item = ~$15.40. Concrete example: CNBC reported a $18.47 dress became $44.68 after $26.21 import charges (142% surcharge). A $12.44 bathing suit became $31.12 (150% fee).

US Supreme Court ruled IEEPA does not authorize emergency tariffs (Feb 20, 2026), but de minimis suspension continues: February 20, 2026: US Supreme Court ruled IEEPA does not authorize the emergency tariffs imposed in 2025. However, executive order continued suspension of duty-free de minimis treatment globally with revised duty rates.

New US law signed July 2025 permanently eliminates de minimis for all commercial shipments from all countries, effective July 1, 2027: A new law signed in July 2025 will eliminate the de minimis exemption for all commercial shipments from all countries, effective July 1, 2027 - marking a permanent, legislative (not executive order) end to low-value import duty exemptions.

Private label brands deliver 25-30% higher gross margins vs national brands, with consumers increasingly accepting quality parity: Private-label sales projected to reach $277B in 2025 (up from $271B in 2024, +3.9% YoY). 74% of consumers say private-label brands are just as good as name brands (2025 Ipsos study). Gen Z private-label adoption growing at ~5% YoY. Premium private labels price just $1-3 below national brands. Private labels deliver 25-30% higher gross margins compared to national brands.

Xinpinmu's pricing model: platform trust premium replaces traditional brand premium, enabling 'quality matching brands, prices below white-label': Chinese media analysis: 'Xinpinmu mechanism essentially uses the platform's trust premium to feed back to the production end' (用平台的信任溢价去反哺生产端). The target is 'quality matching brands, prices below white-label' (品质对标品牌、价格低于白牌). This means Xinpinmu products can be priced HIGHER than generic Temu goods (capturing brand premium) while still being LOWER than established brands.

Higher ASP from Xinpinmu branding dilutes tariff impact as a percentage of product value: Tariff math: If a generic Temu product sells for $5 with 54% tariff = $2.70 duty (54% of price). If a Xinpinmu-branded equivalent sells for $12 with 25% standard duty rate (via bulk import formal entry) = $3.00 duty (25% of price). The absolute duty is similar but the branded product has much higher margin to absorb it. Additionally, bulk import avoids the $100 per-parcel postal fee entirely, which is the true killer for the old model.

Xinpinmu's core strategic insight: move from 'capacity export' to 'industrial capability export' - from low-margin OEM to brand-owner economics: PDD framing: 'pushing Chinese manufacturing from capacity export (产能出海) to industrial capability export (产业能力出海).' Zhao Jiachen (co-CEO): 'Next phase, the company's strategic focus is not business diversification, but fully focusing on high-quality supply chain development.' Chen Lei: 'E-commerce platforms should not be simple transaction platforms, they should do more, creating more value for all participants across the supply chain.'

Temu now operates three parallel models: fully-managed, semi-managed, and third-party (3P) marketplace: Fully-managed: PDD controls pricing, logistics, customer service (original Temu model). Semi-managed: merchant ships to local warehouse, manages own pricing, PDD handles marketplace (launched 2024). Third-party: seller independently selects products, sets prices, ships directly (newest model). Up to 70% of platform traffic is shifting toward Semi-Managed SKUs.

Xinpinmu is a FOURTH model: PDD-owned brand, factory contract manufacturing, PDD handles everything from product definition to after-sales: Xinpinmu is not a replacement for Temu but a complement and 'advanced extension' (进阶与补充). Core difference: Temu continues platform marketplace logic, Xinpinmu integrates Pinduoduo+Temu supply chain resources into brand self-operation. PDD moves from 'matchmaker' (撮合者) to direct participant in manufacturing and brand incubation. Nationwide factories and industrial clusters transition from 'platform recruitment targets' to 'self-operated brand cooperation partners.'

Semi-managed model was the bridge: it taught PDD warehouse operations and local fulfillment, setting the stage for Xinpinmu's 1P model: Timeline of PDD's supply chain evolution: (1) 2022-2024: Fully-managed cross-border model (factory -> PDD air freight -> consumer). (2) 2024: Semi-managed model launches (merchant ships to local warehouse). (3) 2025: US/EU de minimis death forces acceleration of local fulfillment. (4) 2026: Xinpinmu launches as self-operated brand model. Each step added more supply chain capability. Semi-managed taught PDD how to run warehouses; Xinpinmu adds brand ownership and product definition on top.

Temu EU GMV projected to exceed $15B in 2025, potentially $20B by end of 2026; EU accounts for 40% of Temu GMV vs 31% for US: EU market share: 40% of Temu GMV with 80-100% growth. US market: 31% of Temu GMV but growth slowing due to tariff policies. Temu total revenue 2025: ~$92.5B on temu.com. Analysts project operating income of $775M by 2026 with warehouse efficiency gains. Revenue projection: 35-40% increase in 2026.

Xinpinmu also addresses regulatory compliance risk by separating brand operations from marketplace platform: Industry analysts note: 'Xinpinmu's establishment is also a proactive strategy to address compliance pressures during Temu's rapid expansion, achieving risk isolation and functional optimization through business separation' (通过业务分拆实现风险隔离与职能优化). Future structure: Temu platform marketplace + Xinpinmu self-operated brands = complementary, with Temu handling long-tail demand and Xinpinmu setting quality benchmarks.

Temu operates 13 self-owned warehouses globally: 10 EU, 2 US, 1 Mexico (as of Oct 2025): 13 self-owned warehouses: EU (Germany, France, Spain, Netherlands, Italy, Austria, UK - 10 total), US (Rowland Heights CA, Dallas TX - 2), Mexico border ('Jitu Warehouse' - 120,000 sqm - 1). 14th planned for Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Also operates 165,000 sqm logistics center in Gimpo, South Korea.

Temu targets 80% of European orders fulfilled via local warehouses by 2026: Temu announced goal of 80% of all European orders shipped from local warehouses. Started using local warehouses in Europe in December 2024. UK target: 50% local fulfillment by end of 2025. EU warehouse locations: Rotterdam, Frankfurt (largest/most advanced), plus UK, Germany, Austria, Italy, Spain, France.

Temu US self-operated warehouses projected to handle only 15-20% of US volume in 2025, rising to 20-25% by 2026: Self-operated warehouses in US handle 15-20% of total US goods volume (2025 projection), rising to 20-25% by 2026. Self-operated warehousing will NOT become the primary delivery method in the US market. Primary function: storing best-selling products, providing initial support for semi-managed merchants. This suggests Xinpinmu-branded products would be the primary use case for these warehouses.

Amazon operates 150+ US fulfillment centers and 600+ total US logistics sites - dwarfing Temu's 2 US warehouses: Amazon: 150+ US fulfillment centers, 600+ total logistics sites, 1,200+ globally. 35 FCs in California alone, 28 in Texas, 17 in New Jersey. 92% of Prime members within one-day ground zone. $15B plan for 80 new sites in 2025. 750K robotics units. By comparison, Temu has 2 US warehouses. This scale gap means Xinpinmu can only serve a small, curated product selection in the US initially.

Temu partnerships with major logistics providers for European local fulfillment: Temu has forged partnerships with DHL, FedEx, Royal Mail, and bpostgroup for local delivery. This suggests Temu does not need to build last-mile delivery from scratch - it can leverage existing infrastructure. The Xinpinmu model would ship bulk to Temu warehouses, then hand off to these local carriers for delivery.

Xinpinmu introduces costs PDD has NEVER borne: inventory risk, warehousing, brand development, quality control, and working capital for pre-purchasing inventory: Multiple Chinese media analyses highlight: 'Self-operated model involves warehousing, logistics, and quality control, requiring higher cost investment, longer operational cycles, and correspondingly higher risk.' (自营模式涉及仓储、物流和品控，需要更高的成本投入、更长的运营周期，相应地风险也更高). Previously Temu was 100% 3P marketplace with 0% 1P contribution - zero inventory risk. Xinpinmu flips this for its branded products.

RMB 100B (USD 14B) over 3 years is a massive capital commitment, but PDD has RMB 311B ($43B) cash on hand: PDD 2025 full-year: revenue RMB 431.8B (+10% YoY), net profit RMB 99.4B (-12% YoY due to 'thousand-billion subsidy' investment). The RMB 100B/3-year commitment represents roughly 1 year of net profit or ~32% of cash reserves. The -12% profit decline already reflects heavy supply chain investment. PDD's cash position provides substantial runway but this IS a meaningful bet.

PDD's lean operating model (34% operating margin, 17K staff) faces structural challenge in running 1P brand operations at scale: PDD operates with 34% operating margin and only 17,000 staff - far leaner than Alibaba (15% margin) or JD.com (3% margin). Self-operated brands require: product designers, quality inspectors, warehouse operators, brand marketers, IP lawyers, regulatory compliance teams. This represents a fundamental change in PDD's organizational DNA from asset-light platform to asset-heavy brand operator.

Xinpinmu described as 'heavy lifting' work - a departure from PDD's historically asset-light model: 36kr analysis: 'Self-operated model's advantages and disadvantages are both clear. It is the type of heavy, laborious work (搬砖类型的苦活累活). But the advantage lies in barriers - if the self-operated brand is strong enough, it can form a more effective moat.' PDD management framing: 'E-commerce platforms should not be simple transaction platforms, they should and can do more.' This signals PDD accepts the cost/risk tradeoff.

Xinpinmu must absorb unsold inventory risk in 13+ overseas warehouses - a risk PDD has never carried: Under old model, merchants bore all inventory and unsold stock risk. Under Xinpinmu, PDD pre-purchases from factories, ships bulk to 13 self-owned warehouses (10 EU, 2 US, 1 Mexico) plus third-party warehouses. If demand predictions are wrong, PDD eats the loss. For context: Amazon's inventory write-downs in 2022 exceeded $2B when pandemic demand forecasts proved wrong. PDD has no institutional experience managing inventory risk at scale.

Xinpinmu established as separate company in Shanghai, RMB 15B first-tranche investment, RMB 100B over 3 years for self-operated brand building: On March 25, 2026, PDD announced formation of 'Xinpinmu' (新拼姆). Name combines 'Pin' from Pinduoduo and 'mu' from Temu. Registered as a new dedicated company in Shanghai. First cash injection: RMB 15 billion (~$2.1B). Total planned investment over 3 years: RMB 100 billion (~$14B). Described as first step in 'three-year plan to recreate a PDD.'

Xinpinmu fundamentally restructures the value chain: PDD moves from marketplace platform to brand owner and supply chain operator: Core model change: Factories produce under contract (OEM/ODM), PDD owns the brand, defines product standards, handles packaging/branding/overseas marketing/warehousing/logistics/after-sales. Factories earn stable contract manufacturing fees. PDD captures brand premium and supply chain efficiency gains. This is NOT a replacement for Temu - it is a complement. Temu handles long-tail marketplace demand, Xinpinmu builds quality benchmarks with curated self-operated brands.

Xinpinmu value chain for a product sold internationally: Factory -> PDD brand packaging -> bulk ocean shipment -> overseas warehouse -> Temu listing -> local delivery: Three core initiatives: (1) Dedicated company covering domestic industrial belts with customized manufacturing solutions, cultivating self-operated brands across categories; (2) Specialized teams embedded in industrial belts providing integrated product/technology/marketing solutions; (3) Full-service brand overseas solutions including product standards, warehousing logistics, IP services, legal assistance, regulatory compliance review. Factory is responsible for production; PDD handles brand packaging, product definition, overseas marketing, warehousing logistics, after-sales service.

Xinpinmu covers 100+ industrial clusters including Weihai fishing gear, Shenzhen electronics, Pinghu down jackets, Yiwu cosmetics, Shaodong school bags: Specific industrial belts mentioned: Weihai fishing gear (威海钓具), Shenzhen digital/electronics (深圳数码), Pinghu down jackets (平湖羽绒), Yiwu cosmetics (义乌美妆), Shaodong school bags (邵东书包 - produces 70%+ of China's student backpacks). These clusters have mature manufacturing capabilities and standards but lack brand building and global distribution channels - exactly the gap Xinpinmu aims to fill.

Duan Yongping (Step & VIVO founder) compared Xinpinmu to an 'online Costco' with ~4,000 curated SKUs: Duan Yongping stated on Xueqiu: 'This is essentially a Costco. If PDD's online brand has only about 4,000 SKUs, in 10 years it will be formidable.' The Costco comparison implies: extreme SKU curation, low markup, platform trust replacing brand premium, and deep supply chain control. Costco's first 10 years: 1 store to ~200 stores, 10M+ members, all built around 4,000 SKUs.

SCENARIO ANALYSIS — Xinpinmu margin benchmarking: Correct comparables are Anker (44.6% GM, 9.4% OM), UGREEN (37.4% GM, 8.7% OM), Xiaomi IoT (23% GM), Kirkland (~15% markup). NOT JD distributor model (14-19% GM). Xinpinmu as platform-owned brand with factory production should target 20-35% gross margin range.: Margin benchmark matrix for brand-owner models (NOT distributors): Anker Innovations: 44.6% gross margin, 9.38% operating margin (premium DTC, strong brand). UGREEN: 37.4% gross margin, 8.7% operating margin (mid-tier, heavy e-commerce). Xiaomi IoT/Lifestyle: 23-25% gross margin (deliberately low-price positioning, high volume). Kirkland Signature: ~15% markup cap (deliberately cheapest, massive volume). Amazon private label sellers: 30-50% gross margin typical. KEY INSIGHT: Xinpinmu operates factory-direct like Kirkland but with digital distribution like Anker. Expected margin should be 20-35% depending on category and pricing strategy. JD comparison (14-19% gross margin) was WRONG because JD is a distributor buying/selling others' brands, not a brand owner.

Xinpinmu RMB 100B deployment covers three main pillars: (1) customized manufacturing solutions for industrial clusters, (2) integrated product/tech/marketing teams deployed to factories, (3) comprehensive overseas brand solutions including logistics, IP, legal, and regulatory compliance: Three core measures: (1) Establish Xinpinmu dedicated companies, cover domestic industrial belts, launch customized manufacturing plans, incubate self-operated brands across different positioning and categories; (2) Deploy specialized teams directly to industrial belts, leverage platform data advantages, provide integrated product/technology/marketing solutions for merchants; (3) Launch comprehensive overseas brand solutions providing product standards, warehousing logistics, IP services, legal assistance, regulatory review. The RMB 100B is NOT primarily inventory — factories produce, PDD provides brand/standards/distribution. This is a platform-to-brand-owner transition, not a JD-style distributor model.

Xinpinmu model fundamentally differs from JD self-operated: PDD defines brand standards and product specifications but factories produce; PDD doesn't hold inventory in the traditional sense — it's a brand licensor/incubator model closer to Kirkland than to JD: Xinpinmu represents 'moving from helping factories sell goods to engaging in product definition, manufacturing standards, and global marketing through self-operated brand mode.' Factories and industrial clusters transform from 'platform recruitment targets to self-operated brand partners.' PDD becomes 'not just a transaction-matching platform, but a standard-setter and industrial upgrader.' The smile curve principle: helping suppliers move from low-margin OEM to brand ownership. This is NOT JD's distributor model (buy inventory at wholesale, sell at retail). Closer to Kirkland (define specifications, contract manufacture, brand ownership) but with lower overhead (no physical stores, existing traffic).

PDD has already deployed end-to-end delivery infrastructure in 10+ provinces including county-level transfer warehouses and village pickup points, providing existing logistics backbone for Xinpinmu: PDD deployed delivery network in 10+ provinces/municipalities including county-level transfer warehouses and village-level pickup points. PDD subsidizes second-leg delivery costs for rural orders. 'New Quality Supply' already active in industrial clusters: Yiwu accessories, Pinghu down jackets, Hunan spicy snacks, Anhui roasted seeds/nuts, Tianjin potato chips, Zhongshan small appliances, Shanghai chocolate.
